prompt: Create a CineRide Media Operations Platform that unifies a local movie catalog, editorial publishing, and ride-request order handling into one offline-first workstation for small entertainment venues and community shuttles. The interface is built in React and presents three primary workspaces tailored by role: Administrators manage users and system settings; Editors create and schedule content; Reviewers approve or reject with documented reasons; Dispatchers oversee trip requests and resolve disputes; Regular Users browse movies and published content, search, and submit ride/carpool requests. Movie management allows staff to add, edit, and unpublish titles, upload posters and stills (JPG/PNG only, max 10 MB each), and maintain categories, tags, MPAA-style rating, and release dates shown in MM/DD/YYYY. External movie data can be imported from a locally provided file, with an on-screen merge tool that highlights conflicts and lets staff choose field-by-field outcomes while preserving a visible version history. Content publishing supports drafts, scheduled publishing, and revisions for articles, galleries, videos, and events; the UI provides a two-step review chain with required rejection reasons and a sensitive-word warning banner before submission. Users can post ride requests with pickup/drop-off text, rider count (1–6), a time window (up to 4 hours), and vehicle type preference; orders move through pending match, accepted, in progress, completed, canceled, or in dispute, with clear status badges and inline feedback. Cancellation rules are enforced in the UI: free cancellation within 5 minutes of posting, otherwise a dispatcher approval is required; unmatched requests auto-cancel after 30 minutes. Search spans movies, users, and content with query suggestions, typo-tolerant matching, and filters for popularity, rating, and newest, while recommendations personalize results using local behavior signals with a cold-start default of trending movies and editor-curated tags.The backend uses Express to expose REST-style APIs that the React app calls over the local network or localhost, with all data persisted in MongoDB for fully offline operation. Core models include movies with media fingerprints and revision snapshots, content items with draft/publish states and review audit trails, ride requests and orders with immutable state-transition logs, and configuration dictionaries for statuses, tags, and priority values maintained in a local config center (for example, default auto-cancel minutes, dispute escalation windows, and reminder thresholds). Environmental data collection is supported for on-prem sensors via high-frequency sampling (up to 1 sample/second per device) with an offline cache and resumable transfer; the server applies time synchronization rules, deduplicates by device plus timestamp, flags outliers using configurable range limits and spike/drift thresholds, and retains both raw and cleaned versions for 180 days by default. Offline “payment” is implemented as a local funds ledger rather than external channels: payments can be recorded as cash/card-on-file entries with receipt numbers, and the system enforces amount consistency, idempotent handling of duplicate submissions, and reconciliation records that cannot be edited after end-of-day close; failed postings retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff within the app. Security requirements include local username/password login only, salted password hashing, role-based access control, encryption at rest for sensitive fields such as dispute notes and ledger references, and masking on-screen for any stored phone numbers (for example, showing (415) ***-**21). All APIs are documented via locally hosted OpenAPI pages, and extension endpoints are available for optional third-party integration strictly within the same offline environment.
